verses.commentary: Abraham is old. The LORD had blessed him in every way (bakol), in everything. Material, spiritual, relational blessing. Yet one task remains: securing Isaac's future.

Senior servant. In charge of all. Likely Eliezer (15:2). Put your hand under my thigh. An oath gesture, possibly touching the organ of procreation, symbolizing oath on future descendants. Solemn and binding.
Swear by the LORD. God of heaven and earth. Not from the Canaanites. Avoiding intermarriage with idolatrous peoples. Spiritual compatibility matters.
Go to my country, back to Mesopotamia. My own relatives. Family ties and shared faith. Get a wife for Isaac. The mission is clear.
Practical question. What if she refuses? Shall I take Isaac there? Clarifying the limits.
Absolute prohibition. Do not take my son back there. Isaac must stay in the promised land. The future is here, not in Mesopotamia.
Abraham's confidence. The LORD who brought me out. Who promised. He will send his angel. Divine guidance is assured. You can get a wife. Faith in God's provision.
Contingency plan. If she refuses, you're released. But one absolute: do not take Isaac back. The land is non-negotiable.
Oath completed. The gesture. The commitment. Concerning this matter. The mission is sacred.
Ten camels. Loaded with gifts. All kinds of good things. Wealth demonstrates Abraham's blessing. Aram Naharaim, Mesopotamia. Town of Nahor, Abraham's brother.
Strategic positioning. Near the well. Evening, the time women draw water. The servant positions himself where he'll meet eligible women.
Prayer before action. Make me successful. Show kindness (chesed). The servant's faith is evident.
Describing the situation. I am standing. Daughters are coming. The stage is set.

The test. A specific sign. Not just giving him water, but offering to water the camels too. This shows kindness, initiative, and servant-heartedness. Watering ten camels is significant labor (possibly 140 gallons of water). Let her be the one. By this I will know. Faith seeking confirmation.
Before he finished praying. Immediate answer. Rebekah arrives. The genealogy confirms she's from Abraham's family (22:23). Perfect candidate.
Very beautiful. Virgin. No man had slept with her. Her character and purity established. She went to the spring. The scene unfolds.
He acts. Hurries to meet her. Please give me a little water. The test begins.
Her response. Drink, my lord. Quickly. She acts with respect and urgency. Gave him a drink. First part of the test passed.
The clincher. I'll draw water for your camels too. She volunteers. Until they have had enough. Complete service. This is exactly the sign the servant prayed for.
Quickly. Ran. Drew enough for all. She works hard. The servant watches in amazement.
Silent observation. Watched her closely. To learn whether the LORD had made his journey successful. Faith and verification work together.
Lavish gifts. Gold nose ring. Gold bracelets. Specific weights given. Valuable jewelry. He rewards her kindness and signals his purpose.
Key questions. Whose daughter? Verifying family. Is there room? Requesting hospitality. The answers will confirm everything.
Perfect answer. Abraham's great-niece. The family connection confirmed.
Hospitality offered. Plenty of resources. Room for you. Generous welcome.
Immediate worship. Bowed down. The servant recognizes God's hand.
Praise to the LORD. Kindness (chesed) and faithfulness (emet). The LORD has led me. Direct guidance. To the house of my master's relatives. Perfect outcome.
She ran. Told her mother's household. Excitement. The news spreads.
Laban introduced. He will become important later (Jacob's story). Hurried out. Eager response.
Laban sees the jewelry. Material motivation suggested. Heard what the man said. He goes to investigate.
Laban's welcome. You who are blessed by the LORD. Why stand outside? Hospitality offered. House and camels provided for.
Entry into the house. Camels cared for. Water for washing. Full hospitality.
Priority on mission. I will not eat until. Business first. Laban invites him to speak.
Identity established. Representative of Abraham.
Abraham's blessing detailed. Abundant blessing. Wealthy. The list shows comprehensive prosperity. This validates Abraham and makes Isaac an attractive match.
Sarah bore Isaac in old age. The miracle child. He has given him everything. Isaac is the sole heir. The proposition becomes very attractive.
The oath explained. Not from Canaanites. The prohibition makes sense.
Go to my family. Get a wife. The mission stated.
The servant recounts his question. What if she refuses?
Abraham's faith recounted. The LORD will send his angel. Make your journey successful. Divine guidance promised.
The release clause. If they refuse, you're released. Contingency acknowledged.
The prayer at the well recounted. Grant success. Dependence on God.
The test described. Standing by the spring. The request for water.
The sign. Water the camels too. Let her be the one. Specific criteria.
Before I finished praying. Immediate answer. Rebekah appeared. Drew water. The test began.
Her response recounted. Quickly. Drink. I'll water your camels. Perfect fulfillment of the sign.
Family confirmed. Bethuel's daughter. Perfect lineage. Gifts given.
Worship. Praise. The LORD led me. Right road. Granddaughter of Abraham's brother. Everything aligned.
Request for decision. If you will show kindness (chesed) and faithfulness (emet). Tell me yes or no. So I may know. Directness requested.
Recognition of divine will. This is from the LORD. We can say nothing. Submission to God's plan.
Decision made. Take her. Let her become the wife. As the LORD has directed. Full consent.
Worship again. Bowed to the ground. Gratitude for answered prayer.
Lavish gifts. Gold, silver, jewelry, clothing for Rebekah. Costly gifts for family. Bride price and honor gifts.
Finally eats. Spends the night. Next morning, urgency returns. Send me on my way. Mission accomplished, time to return.
Delay requested. Ten days or so. Normal for family to keep her longer.
Servant insists. Do not detain me. The LORD has granted success. Let me return. Urgency motivated by mission completion.
Wise decision. Let's ask her. Rebekah's voice matters.
Rebekah's choice. Will you go? I will go. Decisive, immediate. Faith and courage.
Rebekah departs. Her nurse accompanies her. The journey begins.
Family blessing. May you increase. Thousands upon thousands. May your offspring possess. Prophetic blessing echoing God's promises to Abraham.
They depart. Mounted camels. Went with the man. The servant takes Rebekah. Homeward journey.
Isaac introduced. From Beer Lahai Roi, the well where Hagar met God (16:14). Living in the Negev. The stage is set for meeting.
Isaac went to the field. To meditate (lasuach), possibly to pray or reflect. Evening. Looked up. Saw camels. The moment approaches.
Rebekah sees Isaac. She dismounts. Respect and perhaps recognition.
Who is that man? He is my master. She takes her veil. Modesty, respect, anticipation.
Full report. The servant recounts the journey. Everything explained.

Into Sarah's tent. Symbolic replacement. He married her. She became his wife. He loved her. Love after marriage, not before. And Isaac was comforted. His grief over Sarah finds healing in Rebekah. The chapter ends with love and comfort.
